summary: Typosquat of polymarket-sdk exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet/seed files, and secrets to Telegram on install
details: @wagni_bot/polymarket-sdk is a typosquat of the legitimate polymarket-sdk package. Its index.js is an empty stub (module.exports = {}); the only real code is postinstall.js, which runs automatically on npm install. On execution it recursively scans process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching keywords including.env,.cred,.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519, config, mnemonic, seed, wallet, and secret, reads their contents, and uploads them in ~3000-character chunks via HTTPS POST to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage against chat_id 892359416. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), platform, cwd, and every environment variable whose name contains PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS, and sends those to the same Telegram bot. The bot token and chat id are hardcoded attacker infrastructure. Installing this package results in immediate theft of SSH private keys, cryptocurrency wallet/seed material, cloud credentials, and secret-bearing environment variables from the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
